demographics.living_situation: supported residential accommodation (group home) with support workers

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Hi, I'm a support worker calling about one of our residents. He's got really sore, itchy spots on his face and back and he won't stop scratching them — now some of them are bleeding and he's very upset and agitated. I'm not sure how to settle him or look after the raw bits.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The scratching and bleeding have been going on for hours tonight and he's getting more distressed — I want to help settle it and I'm worried about the raw spots before the morning.

symptom_narrative.onset: the spots flared up over the last three days, and he's been scratching them non-stop for the last few hours tonight
symptom_narrative.duration: worse over about 3 days, with constant scratching for roughly the last 6 hours
symptom_narrative.character: really itchy and sore; some spots are raw and bleeding where he's been scratching, and a couple are weeping a bit
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: very distressing for him — he can't leave them alone and it's disrupted his whole evening and his sleep
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: his face, along his jaw, and his upper back and chest
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing seems to help — he keeps going back to picking at them
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: the more he scratches and picks, the more they bleed
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: his skin is very oily, and he's agitated and pacing around the room
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he's had breakouts before but nothing that got him this worked up or bleeding like this
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he can't settle, won't do his evening routine, and isn't sleeping

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: intellectual disability — he needs support with a lot of daily tasks

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: nothing regular for his skin — no creams or tablets that I know of

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: he lives in a supported group home and support workers provide his care; the home is set up for his needs and sharp objects are kept out of his reach

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: The support worker wants practical, concrete help to settle the person and stop the bleeding and picking tonight, plus reassurance about whether this needs urgent in-person care.

communication_style.typical_message_style: carer speaking on the patient's behalf; describes what she can see and what the resident is doing, asks practical how-to questions

objective_data_offered.value: lots of blackheads, whiteheads and red pus-topped spots across his face and upper back, and his skin looks very oily
fresh scratch marks over the spots along his jaw, with tiny points of bleeding and a bit of weeping/oozing
he's agitated, pacing the room and keeps touching and picking at his face
he doesn't feel hot to me and seems his normal self temperature-wise
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: carer's impression by touch only — no thermometer reading taken
